transcript: Um, today I want to talk about, you know, the idea that tiny habits can, like, compound into really big results. Basically, what I've learned is that, uh, you don't rise to the level of your goals; you fall to the level of your systems. Actually, the deepest system of all is identity. Kind of, you have to decide what kind of person you want to become, right? So the four laws are: make it obvious, make it attractive, make it easy, make it satisfying. Sort of, the most overlooked is the second one. You know, if you pair the new behavior with something you already enjoy, your brain starts to anticipate the reward. Like, that's the seed of consistency. Okay so, once the habit is anchored, the rest is patience. I mean, you're not chasing the outcome anymore. You're just showing up. Right? That's the whole idea.

Actually, the deepest system of all is identity. Kind of, you have to decide what kind of person you want to become, right?
So the four laws are: make it obvious, make it attractive, make it easy, make it satisfying. Sort of, the most overlooked is the second one.
You know, if you pair the new behavior with something you already enjoy, your brain starts to anticipate the reward.
Like, that's the seed of consistency. Okay so, once the habit is anchored, the rest is patience.
I mean, you're not chasing the outcome anymore. You're just showing up. Right? That's the whole idea.
